title: SSRF: inbox URI from actor doc not validated before Accept send
description: server.rs handle_inbox (Follow branch): the SSRF guard (is_private_host) runs on follower_actor before fetching the actor document, but the inbox_uri extracted from that document (actor_doc['inbox']) is used directly in a POST without any SSRF validation. An attacker controls https://legit.example/actor.json and can serve {"inbox": "http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/"} or "http://192.168.1.1/admin". The outbound Accept POST then hits the metadata endpoint or internal network. Pass 2 added SSRF protection on the actor fetch URL but missed the second outbound request. Fix: run is_private_host (and https-only check) on inbox_uri before the Accept POST, matching the guard already applied to follower_actor.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
